name: Hace
description_short: HACE is a Philadelphia-based nonprofit CDC offering free housing counseling, tax preparation, and community development services to low-to-moderate income residents in North Philadelphia since 1982.
description_long:
HACE (Housing Association for Community Enterprise) was established in 1982 as a community development corporation focused on building equitable neighborhoods in Eastern North Philadelphia. The organization has evolved into a comprehensive community service provider addressing housing, economic development, and financial wellness for low-to-moderate income families and small business owners in the region.

HACE's primary service offerings include HUD-approved housing counseling, free tax preparation for qualifying households, and homeownership programs through their affiliated Good Lands Trust community land trust. The organization provides one-on-one counseling and educational workshops on budgeting, homeownership, and financial planning. Beyond direct financial services, HACE engages in community development through affordable housing preservation, commercial space development, small business support, and neighborhood revitalization projects.

What distinguishes HACE is its dual focus on financial counseling paired with community-level economic development. Rather than operating solely as a credit counselor, HACE integrates financial education with broader neighborhood stability initiatives—community gardens, affordable housing development, and small business growth. The organization's Good Lands Trust model specifically addresses displacement risk in gentrifying areas by making properties available to low-income homebuyers at affordable prices, with homes purchased through CLTs being ten times less likely to fall into foreclosure.

HACE operates as a legitimate 501(c)(3) nonprofit with demonstrated community presence and partnerships (Rebuilding Together Philadelphia, People's Budget Office, Smart Growth America). However, potential clients should note that services appear geographically limited to North Philadelphia/Fairhill neighborhoods, free tax preparation has income eligibility requirements, and the organization's primary strength lies in housing counseling rather than comprehensive credit repair or debt resolution services.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling staff providing professional guidance on homeownership and financial planning
Free tax preparation services for qualifying low-to-moderate income households during tax season
Good Lands Trust community land trust model with proven outcomes—homes 10x less likely to foreclose, 79% first-time homebuyers
Established 501(c)(3) nonprofit with 40+ years operating history (since 1982) and strong community partnerships
Integrated approach combining financial counseling with neighborhood development and affordability preservation
Accessible contact methods (two phone numbers provided: 215-426-8025 and 215-437-7867) for service inquiries
Staff leadership actively engaged in policy discussions and community equity work at national level
cons: Services appear geographically limited to Eastern North Philadelphia/Fairhill area—not a regional or national provider
Free tax preparation has income eligibility requirements that exclude some households
Website provides limited detail on specific counseling services, eligibility criteria, or program outcomes beyond housing focus
No indication of debt resolution, credit repair, or credit building loan programs—primarily housing-focused
Limited information on counselor credentials, wait times, or service availability/hours
best_for: First-time homebuyers in North Philadelphia seeking affordable housing and homeownership counseling
Low-to-moderate income households needing free tax preparation services during tax season
Residents of gentrifying Philadelphia neighborhoods interested in community land trust homeownership models
Small business owners and community members seeking neighborhood economic development resources
diagnosis: HACE is best suited for low-to-moderate income residents in North Philadelphia seeking free housing counseling, tax preparation, and pathways to affordable homeownership through a community land trust model. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to Eastern North Philadelphia and a housing-focused mission rather than comprehensive credit repair, debt resolution, or credit building services.
services: HUD-approved housing counseling and homeownership education
Free tax preparation for qualifying low-to-moderate income households
Good Lands Trust community land trust homeownership programs
Affordable housing development and property acquisition/redevelopment
Housing preservation and home maintenance support programs
Commercial space development for small businesses
Small business support and economic development services
Community engagement and resident participation programs
People's Budget workshops and civic engagement education
